candidates.stmt: I’m standing for Bedhampton on Havant Borough Council to protect our community, green spaces and local heritage, while standing up for residents and demanding better local services. Bedhampton deserves practical leadership, common sense and a councillor who will always put local people first. I want to make a positive difference.

Isabel Harrison believes in fairness and equality of opportunity for all, with people’s issues being supported through local government, not through the central state. Only by understanding people’s concerns at a local level and appointing passionate voices from within the community as their representatives can we make a difference and mend this ailing country.

As a Liberal Democrat and a Marathon runner, Isabel is appalled by the ongoing damage to our local environment, most clearly exemplified by sewage being dumped in our waterways across the country including Langstone Harbour and Southsea. To that end, she believes we must put an end to dumping sewage and must instead focus on enhancing our environmental record through planting trees, improving cycling routes and investing in recycling provisions.

Isabel also hopes to take on the housing crisis in our local area. She supports the improvement of infrastructure and the building of new homes particularly so younger people can get on the housing ladder. She also wishes to bring further investment into local shops to encourage our local economy.

Isabel moved to Portsmouth in 2012 and Purbrook in 2015 after living in Spain and Belgium for 9 years. She was educated at Purbrook Park School.

I am the third generation of our family to live in Waterlooville and consider it home. I bought my first house here nearly 20 years ago, so my family and I have witnessed first hand the decline in our services and High Street.

Most of my working life has been spent as a plumber although I moved up the ranks to become a senior mechanical engineer in building services for a company in Waterlooville. This has given me a wealth of practical experience including project and contract management, so I have a unique view on delivering solutions to real world problems.

In my spare time, and for the last 12 years, I have been part of a children's motorcycle display team. I began as an assistant, worked my way up to chairman and was also senior trainer handling local training sessions and public performances. From this I learned about teaching, leadership, and public speaking - and the pleasure of giving back to my community.

I am also heavily involved in renewable technologies that actually save money without the need to pursue the mantra of net zero. Common sense and cost effective solutions to everyday challenges.

If elected, my objective will be to make every pound of taxpayer money count. I will root out inefficiencies and pay particular attention to contracts and how much value they deliver. I want to see the effective regeneration of our town centre, projects that add value to our community and more. I want to see radical improvements in the quality of road maintenance with a greater focus on long term solutions rather than ineffective quick fixes.

Finding real solutions to our problems needs effective coordination between local and county representatives and this is why I am standing as both your local borough candidate and also your county council candidate. I promise commitment, effective communication and fresh ideas.

I settled in the Havant area with my family 15 years ago, having lived all over the country growing up. We have made the area our home.

When asked what I do for a living, I usually have two responses ready. The first, I work in IT. The second, I take facts, figures and statistics, and analyse them to present the truth. I have worked all over the country in many different sectors, from banking to social services.

My personal moral compass points to equality, accountability, and personal responsibility. Hart Plain deserves a councillor who cares about the area and the people in it.

Dan has been a resident of Havant for the past 13 years, where he and his wife have been raising their three children who all go to local Havant schools.

Dan explains: ‘Like most residents, I share many of our frustrations when it comes to our ward. Important issues include; the poor state of our footbridges, the unacceptable situation with sewage dumping including the latest reservoir proposal, the amount of dog mess on our streets, the growing traffic queues at our train gates, planning issues with new builds in the area, and many others.

In my career I manage quality assurance teams. This aligns with my belief and passion that everyone should be entitled to high quality products and outcomes, a passion I also hold to our vital public services, and the area we all live in day to day. I believe in a working council that co-operates for the greater good of the community and the area.

I’m standing because Hayling is a special place that’s being overlooked and taken for granted, and it deserves a stronger voice representing the residents.
I’ve lived here for 18 years and visited for over 50, and I care deeply about protecting the island for Islanders, and future generations of Islanders, visitors and wildlife.
My priorities:
🌊 Deliver the promised sea defences
💧 Tackle sewage pollution and protect our coastline
🌳 Protect community spaces and green areas
🏡 Prioritise homes for local people, not overdevelopment
♻️ Promote respectful, sustainable tourism
🚌 Improve local transport links
This isn’t just about promises - it’s about taking action and being a strong voice for Islanders.

Hayling Island is special. And for too long, it has been ignored.

Overlooked by the Borough. Overlooked by the County. While other areas got attention, Hayling got left behind.

I have lived and worked here for over 18 years, but Hayling has been in my heart since first visiting 35 years ago. This island is my home, my community, my passion. And that is exactly why I am fighting for it.

Two years as your Councillor has shown me what is possible when someone actually stands up for Hayling. Now I want to do more.

My priorities:

🌊 Deliver the replacement sea defences at West Beach and the South Hayling Sea Defence Scheme

💧 Hold Southern Water to account over continuous sewage discharges

🚧 Take real action on the pothole epidemic

🚗 Properly scrutinise traffic control on our main access road

🏡 Continue fighting to stop major development on the Island

Two years ago I made you a promise. My record shows I kept it. Now I am asking for the chance to go further, fight harder, and make sure Hayling Island finally gets the voice it deserves.

Vote Jonathan Hulls. For Hayling. For all of us.

I’m standing in Leigh Park Hermitage, because I couldn’t put up with us being ignored and left to the wayside for years. I’m running on issues that I’ve heard directly from residents.

I’ve lived around here since I was young, and I’m raising a family here too. I care about the people here and know we deserve better.

My priorities:

🗑️ Tackle the fly tipping

💧Stand up for the health of our environment and our community

🚘 Maintain and rebuild local infrastructure

🧒 Build stronger community ties

🏡 Help deal with negligent landlords

We deserve better. The people here already work hard for their families, and I intend to do the same for them.

I have lived in Purbrook ward for the last 10 years and within the local area since 2006. Prior to this I have lived in Cairo, London and Aldershot. I am a Teacher in a Havant school and my daughter is currently attending a Havant secondary school. I am passionate about education and want the very best for our young people before, during and after their school years. In Havant I am very concerned that the Conservative majority of councillors have become complacent and take being re-election for granted. I want to see more opposition on the council to hold them to account. I think the best way to do this is with more Labour councillors. My priority is new homes. We need more affordable housing but this needs to be done with care for the environment and without building on the beautiful countryside that surrounds Purbrook. We need to make sure Havant council is run for the needs of all. Only more Labour councillors can make sure this happens.

Being the proud daughter of a Royal Marine, I have had the privilege of living in many places - both home and abroad. 
Moving into Waterlooville in 2001, I now know I have found the ideal place for me and my family. Living in a county such as Hampshire is a delight, we have the Downs, the coast and everything in between.

I would like to thank everyone who has supported me since 2019 as your local representative; it has been a privilege to serve you. With your endorsement, I hope to continue to represent you as your County Councillor and Havant Borough Council representative.

You have told me what your priorities are, as your councillor and a resident myself I understand these; our streets to be kept clean and safe, council tax to be spent wisely and effectively and the regeneration of Waterlooville Town Centre to be completed as promised.

With your support, I will continue to challenge decisions which don’t represent the interests of local residents, put Waterlooville front and centre, support businesses and be the strong voice you want and need for our community.
